displayTitle: Circular Fields: Flux Around, Flaws Along

beats.narration: Run current through a conductor and circular flux wraps it. Your right hand's curl shows which way. That's circular magnetization.

payload.title: CIRCULAR FIELD
payload.sub: A magnetic field the instant current flows.

beats.narration: Direct induction sends current through the part: head shot or hand-held prods. Indirect: a central conductor through the bore. No contact, no arc burn.
Flux must cross a flaw to force leakage. Circular flux wraps the circumference, lighting up seams and quench cracks running along the axis.
A circular field hides inside the part: no external poles. So prove it: pie gauge, QQI shim, flaw side to the part, or a gaussmeter.

payload.heading: Prove the field at the surface

chips.t: QQI shim — flaw side to the part

beats.narration: Flux runs around, so it finds flaws that run along. And a field you can't see is a field you prove.
